title: Malaxis chlorophrys
taxonomy: Malaxis chlorophrys is a species of orchid, specifically belonging to the subfamily Orchidoideae. It is classified within the genus Malaxis, which is a group of terrestrial orchids. The genus Malaxis is further divided into several species, with Malaxis chlorophrys being one of them. Malaxis chlorophrys is typically classified as Malaxis chlorophrys (Lindl.) in the plant taxonomy system. In the APG III system, Malaxis chlorophrys is placed in the family Orchidaceae.
uses: Malaxis chlorophrys is a species of orchid that is used in traditional medicine in certain regions. Its roots are used to treat fever and inflammation. intro: Malaxis chlorophrys, also known as Green Wood Orchid, is a species of orchid native to tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, infections, and respiratory issues.
